title: Mailchimp vs MailerLite (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and MailerLite. See pricing, features, ratings, and which email marketing tool wins for your business.
h1: Mailchimp vs MailerLite: A Complete Comparison
intro: Both Mailchimp and MailerLite are popular email marketing platforms offering free tiers and affordable paid plans. If you're deciding between these two tools, this comparison will help you understand their strengths, weaknesses, and which is better suited to your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You're an established business needing advanced integrations and a well-known platform name
You require extensive third-party app connections beyond email marketing
You prefer a platform with the longest track record (founded in 2001)
choose_b_if: You want the best value for money with a lower starting price ($9 vs $13)
You need built-in pop-ups, surveys, and e-commerce features out of the box
You're a small business or freelancer prioritizing ease of use and customer support quality
You want a larger free tier (1,000 vs 500 subscribers) before upgrading

faq.a: MailerLite generally offers better value, with lower pricing, higher subscriber limits on the free tier, and superior customer reviews on Trustpilot (4.5 vs 1.5). However, Mailchimp may be preferable for larger enterprises needing extensive integrations. For most small businesses and freelancers, MailerLite is the better choice.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or MailerLite?
faq.a: MailerLite is cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to Mailchimp's $13/month. Additionally, MailerLite offers a larger free tier with 1,000 subscribers versus Mailchimp's 500, making it more budget-friendly for startups and small businesses.

faq.q: Can I switch from MailerLite to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber lists between the two platforms. Most email marketing tools support CSV exports and imports, allowing you to transfer your contacts, campaigns, and data. Both Mailchimp and MailerLite provide documentation for migration, though you may need to rebuild automation workflows manually.
